summary: Multi-dimensional administrative register from Nazca nicknamed 'Valhalla' — the MOST COLORFUL khipu in the OKR corpus with 21 unique colors. 866 cords, tree structure (L2/L1=1.61). chiqa (truth/verification) dominates (x9 = 26% of STRING), suggesting an INSPECTION or AUDIT register. Geographic terms: qaqa x6, kaqa x2, chaqa x1. Aymara-zone markers confirm Nazca provenance. HEAVILY DAMAGED — multiple cords repaired, stitched, fragile. 34 STRING, ~53% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  866 cords (L1: 332, L2: 505, L3: 28, L4: 1). Architecture: 0-SEUL but L2/L1 ratio = 1.61 (more subsidiaries than mains).
  21 UNIQUE COLORS — THE MOST COLORFUL KHIPU IN THE CORPUS.
  Nickname: 'Valhalla'. 31 clusters (sizes 4-20).
  Provenance: Nazca — southern coast, Aymara zone.
  Museum: Museum for World Culture, Goteborg, Sweden.
  HEAVILY DAMAGED — cords 292-295 'fragile and disintegrating', cord 69 'not attached, location uncertain', cord 88 'patched, legitimacy dubious', cord 22 'stitched with modern thread', cord 33 'knotted like a noose'.
  34 STRING (4% of cords), 685 INT, 143 EMPTY (17%).
  kvt_bug = approximate numbers only.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR113 is a multi-dimensional administrative register from Nazca, using 21 colors to encode at least 8-10 data categories simultaneously. chiqa (truth/right/verification) dominates (x9 = 26%), suggesting an INSPECTION or AUDIT register — a document that records what was verified as true. However, significant physical damage means the reading is partial and tentative.

KEY VOCABULARY
  chiqa (truth/verification x9) — DOMINANT word, 26% of STRING
  qaqa (rock/mountain x6) — geography
  qaki (unglossed x4) — frost? dry?
  kaqa (wall/cliff-AY x2) — geography
  maki (hand/work x2) — labor
  chaqa (gorge/bridge x1), maqa (strike/eclipse x1), mapa (wax-AY x1)
  pay (he/she x1), kapa (noble x1), kay (to be x1)
  Compounds: qaqaqa (rock-TOP x1), qakiqa (qaki-TOP x1), qakiqaki (doubled x1)
  3 Aymara words (kaqa, mapa) confirm Nazca = Aymara zone.

COLORS — 21 UNIQUE (RECORD)
  W(205), AB(170), RB(149), KB(128), YB(55), MB(39), GG(29), G0(21), plus 13 more rare colors.
  Previous record: UR203 with 10 colors. Valhalla doubles that.
  Each color = one data dimension. The most information-dense khipu in existence.
  Goteborg museum research: 'khipus contain much more information than just their knotted structure — a great part resides in their colors.'

STRUCTURE
  31 clusters, varying sizes (4-20 cords). No single dominant size.
  L2/L1 ratio = 1.61 — each main cord has 1-2 subsidiaries = TREE structure.
  L1 = category/label, L2 = detailed data.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR113 vs UR203 (Pachacamac ceque, 10 colors): UR113 far more complex. UR203 = ceque/huaca registry, UR113 = audit/inspection register.
  UR113 vs UR198 (Nazca census, 3 colors): Both from Nazca but dramatically different complexity.
  Nazca connection: UR113 suggests Nazca used the most complex color-coding of any khipu-producing culture.

DATA QUALITY CAVEAT
  UR113 is the most DAMAGED khipu analyzed. At least 13 cords have documented issues. Any interpretation is TENTATIVE pending physical re-examination.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Multi-dimensional register analysis, 21-color record
Museum for World Culture, Goteborg, Sweden
Heritage Science: Understanding Meaningful Khipu Colours (MDPI, 2023) — khipu dye analysis
Cross-corpus: UR203 (ceque registry, 10 colors), UR198 (Nazca census, 3 colors)
Nazca administrative culture — southern coast Aymara zone
